description: Implement the merge engine in internal/merge/. Define the Merger interface and implement the sequential merge strategy. Sequential merges happen in a dedicated temporary worktree (not the user's working directory), use a file lock for mutual exclusion, and update the target ref via git update-ref. Reference docs/implementation-plan.md Milestone 8a and 8b for the interface, MergeRequest/MergeResult structs, and the full sequential merge algorithm.
commit: feat(merge): add merge engine with sequential strategy
steps: Create internal/merge/merge.go with Merger interface and MergeRequest/MergeResult structs

Create internal/merge/sequential.go: implement SequentialMerger. Acquire file lock (.flywheel/merge.lock). Create or reuse merge worktree at .flywheel/merge-workspace checked out to targetRef. Run git merge {branch} --no-ff. If clean: record merge commit SHA, update real ref via git update-ref, return success. If conflicts: parse conflict files from git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U, abort merge, return HasConflicts with file list. Release lock in defer.

Create internal/merge/merge_test.go using temp git repos: test clean merge updates base_ref and doesn't touch user's working directory, test conflict detection with conflicting changes and clean abort, test merge lock prevents concurrent merges (goroutine test), test merge worktree reuse across sequential merges
acceptance_criteria: Merger interface has a single Merge method
Sequential merge uses a dedicated worktree, never the user's working directory
File lock ensures only one merge at a time
Clean merges use --no-ff and update the target ref via git update-ref
Conflicting merges are detected, aborted cleanly, and report conflict file list
Merge worktree is reused across sequential merges
All tests pass with real git operations
